question.text: Given USD/JPY = 115.08/115.09 and USD/INR = 75.64/75.65, what is the offer rate for JPY/INR (per 100 JPY)?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** To BUY JPY: (1) Buy USD at 75.65 INR (offer). (2) Sell USD for JPY at 115.08 (bid), getting 115.08 JPY per USD. Price of 100 JPY = 75.65/115.08 × 100 = 65.74 INR. **The Trap:** For JPY/INR offer, use USD/INR offer ÷ USD/JPY bid (not the other way around). **Cross-Exam Context:** This calculation is frequently tested in Series I exams. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** When deriving cross rates where USD is base in both pairs, remember to divide and flip the bid/offer usage.
